memoryArtifacts.description: Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Greasley, Gresley, Grysley, Grisle, Grysely, Grisley, Grelly, Gresly, Greseley, Greiseley, Grelley and many more. First found in Derbyshire where they were anciently seated as Lords of the Manor. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Jeffery Gresley, who arrived in Virginia in 1791; as well as Philip J Greaseley, who was Naturalized in Fairfield Co. Ohio in 1833. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009) This interesting surname is of Anglo-Saxon origin, and is a locational name from Gresley in Derbyshire, recorded as "Griseleia" in the 1130 Pipe Rolls, or from Greasley in Nottinghamshire, recorded as "Griseleia" in the Domesday Book of 1086. The place name derives from the Old English pre-7th Century "greosn", gravel, with "leah", wood, clearing; hence "gravelly clearing". Locational surnames were developed when former inhabitants of a place moved to another area, usually to seek work, and were best identified by the name of their birthplace. William de Greseleia is noted in the Staffordshire Chartulary (1130). The surname may also be of Norman origin, introduced into England after the Conquest of 1066 C.E., and is an example of that large group of European surnames that were gradually created by the habitual use of a nickname. In this case the name was originally given to someone with a pitted or pock-marked face, possibly to some of those few survivors of the frequent epidemics of plague in the Middle Ages in Europe. The name derives from the Old Norman-French "gresle", "greslet", pitted, scarred. Albert Gresley is listed in the Records of St. Benet of Holme, Norfolk (1153). The surname can also be found as Gresley and Gresly. On 15 February 1633, Elizabeth Greasley married Thomas Ward at Radford, Nottinghamshire. The Coat of Arms most associated with the family is a shield vaire ermine and red, the Crest being an ermine lion passant, armed, langued and collared gold. The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of Albert Greslet, which was dated 1086, in the "Domesday Book of Cheshire", during the reign of King William 1st, known as "The Conqueror", 1066 - 1087. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 – 2015.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomasine de Gresley於大約1410年出生在英國德比夏爾時，她的父親John Gresley26歲，母親Elizabeth Clarell21歲。她於1430年與John Darrel在英國英格蘭越可爾結婚。她去世於1480年12月25日在她的家鄉享年71歲。

meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Gresley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Greasley
meanings: 1  English: locative name, usually from Greasley (Notts) but possibly also from Gresley (Derbys). Some of the following early bearers may alternatively belong under (2), particularly the Lancs examples.

2 Norman, English: nickname, either from Old French greslet ‘marked as by hail’, i.e. pitted or pock-marked, or Old French greslet, gresli, grailet ‘thin, slim’. In early forms, the nickname has sometimes been confused with the place-name in (1). Compare Le Gresley in Jersey and Greslé, Grêlé in France.

nameMeanings.name: Thomasine
meanings: Hebrew: A twin; Thomasine is a female version.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomasine de Gresley was born about 1410, in Derbyshire, England, her father, John Gresley, was 26 and her mother, Elizabeth Clarell, was 21. She married John Darrel in 1430, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. She died on 25 December 1480, in her hometown, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomasine de Gresley est née environ 1410, à Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, John Gresley, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Clarell, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé John Darrel en 1430, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle est décédée le 25 décembre 1480, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 71 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomasine de Gresley um 1410 in Derbyshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Gresley 26 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Clarell 21. Sie hat 1430 in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom John Darrel geheiratet. Sie starb am 25. Dezember 1480 im Alter von 71 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomasine de Gresley è nata il circa 1410 nel Derbyshire, England, sua padre, John Gresley, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Clarell, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato John Darrel nel 1430, nel Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì il 25 dicembre 1480, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 71 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomasine de Gresley(이)가 대략 1410년 영국 더비실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Gresley(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Clarell(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1430년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 John Darrel(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1480년 12월 25일 그녀의 고향에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomasine de Gresley родилась около 1410 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Gresley было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Clarell, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Darrel в 1430 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. Она умерла 25 декабря 1480 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 71 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomasine de Gresley nació aproximadamente 1410, en Derbyshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Gresley, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Clarell, tenía 21 años. Se casó con John Darrel en 1430, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió el 25 de diciembre de 1480, en su pueblo, a la edad de 71 años.
